channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Albanese Government Faces Pressure Over NDIS Cuts and Fossil Fuel Support
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's government is under fire for its handling of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) and its continued support of the fossil fuel industry. In a striking reve...

item.title: Tinubu Meets APC Governors Following Emergency Talks With Senate Leadership
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu dey currently hold closed-door meeting with governors wey belong to the All Progressives Congress (APC) for State House, Abuja. The meeting wey dem first plan for 4 p.m. la...

item.title: Rueben Bain Selected 15th Overall in NFL Draft Despite Physical and Personal Challenges
item.description: Rueben Bain Jr., a standout defensive end from the Miami Hurricanes, was selected 15th overall by the Tampa Bay Buccaneers in the 2026 NFL Draft. Despite being a consensus All-American and ACC Defensi...

item.title: Bail Application of Lekompo Artist Shebeshxt Continues at Polokwane Court
item.description: The bail application for Lehlogonolo 'Shebeshxt' Chauke, a well-known and controversial Lekompo musician, continued on Thursday at the Polokwane Magistrate's Court. Shebeshxt, who has previously been ...

item.title: Porsche to Release All-Electric Cayenne Coupe with Multiple Variants
item.description: Porsche has announced that it will launch an all-electric version of its Cayenne coupe in late summer 2026. The Cayenne Coupe Electric, a four-door variant, will join the lineup of Porsche's electric ...

item.title: Coco Martin's First Teleserye Appearance
item.description: Coco Martin, a well-known Filipino actor famously called the 'Primetime King,' began his television career many years ago. His first teleserye marked the start of a remarkable journey in Philippine te...

item.title: Rising fuel costs disrupt transport and education access for special needs children in Sindh
item.description: A growing fuel crisis in Pakistan is seriously affecting the education of children with special needs, particularly those relying on organised transport services. The report highlights the case of Gul...

item.title: Education Ministry Clarifies Validity of KIM Certificates After 2018
item.description: The Ministry of Education in Kenya has clarified the validity of certificates awarded by the Kenya Institute of Management (KIM) since 2018. This clarification came after concerns were raised by the T...

item.title: Farage outlines plans for major welfare cuts and warns of potential public backlash
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, has set out an ambitious and controversial plan to significantly reduce Britain’s welfare spending, warning that such reforms could provoke protests, strikes and eve...

item.title: Kiambu Woman Reflects Personal Growth and Wellbeing After Ending Marriage
item.description: A 39-year-old woman from Kiambu has gone viral after sharing a dramatic transformation following her exit from a marriage she described as toxic. The side-by-side photos, taken 14 years apart, show a ...

item.title: Reforming Provincial Boundaries for Better Governance in Pakistan
item.description: The debate over creating smaller provinces in Pakistan is often tied to ethnicity and historical context, but the real issue lies in the country's governance failure. Pakistan has struggled with equit...

item.title: Man jailed for deliberately infecting victims with HIV as survivor speaks out
item.description: A young man, referred to as Sam to protect his identity, has spoken about the lasting impact of being deliberately infected with HIV at the age of 15 by a man who also raped him. Sam recalls receiving...

item.title: Decline of Classical Greek Studies at Xavier College and Across Victoria
item.description: Victorian students interested in studying classical Greek face dwindling opportunities as Xavier College in Kew ends its 147-year tradition of offering the subject due to persistently low enrolments. ...

item.title: Planned Gas-Powered Data Centers in the U.S. May Significantly Increase Greenhouse Gas Emissions
item.description: A recent report highlights that just 11 planned gas-powered data center campuses in the United States could produce more greenhouse gas emissions than Morocco did in 2024. These projects, associated w...

item.title: HUD halts enforcement of gender identity housing rule, citing focus on biological sex
item.description: Housing and Urban Development (HUD) Secretary Scott Turner announced that the Trump administration will stop enforcing the Obama-era Equal Access Rule of 2016, which required federally-funded housing ...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Reconciliation Commission and Criminal Investigation Following October 29 Violence
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan of Tanzania has set in motion a comprehensive plan to restore national unity and address the consequences of the violent incidents that occurred on October 29. Speaking a...

item.title: Impact of Development Plans on Islamabad’s Population and Environment
item.description: Islamabad, long considered Pakistan’s elite and scenic capital, is facing dramatic changes under the current government’s development agenda. The plans for a 'new' Islamabad promise a city that blends...

item.title: Rising Gulf tensions and shifting capital flows threaten global financial stability
item.description: The article argues that escalating tensions in the Gulf, driven in part by US policy towards Iran under Donald Trump, are creating serious risks for global financial stability. A key concern is the po...

item.title: Eala and Sönmez advance to Madrid Open doubles round of 16 after solid performance
item.description: Filipina tennis standout Alex Eala, together with her doubles partner Zeynep Sönmez, successfully advanced to the round of 16 in the Madrid Open women’s doubles event. The pair delivered a composed an...

item.title: Lawal, Tinubu deliberate on security challenges and economic growth in Zamfara
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State meet with President Bola Ahmed Tinubu for inside Aso Rock Villa, Abuja, to discuss important matters wey concern security, economy and political situation for the...
